citations.title: The prevalence of 'alexithymic' characteristics in psychosomatic patients
citations.description: Original paper coining the term 'alexithymia' and describing the condition characterized by difficulty identifying and describing emotions.

citations.title: Disorders of affect regulation: Alexithymia in medical and psychiatric illness
citations.description: Comprehensive academic treatment of alexithymia, covering assessment, clinical applications, and theoretical frameworks.

citations.title: The Emotions
citations.description: Foundational work on emotion theory introducing Plutchik's Wheel of Emotions and the eight primary emotions framework.

citations.title: Emotions in the Human Face
citations.description: Seminal work establishing the six basic emotions and their universal facial expressions across cultures.

citations.title: Ethics
citations.description: Philosophical treatise establishing three primary affects (joy, sadness, desire) as the foundation of all emotions.

citations.title: The Expression of the Emotions in Man and Animals
citations.description: Evolutionary analysis of emotional expression, establishing the biological basis of emotions across species.

citations.title: Atlas of the Heart: Mapping Meaningful Connection and the Language of Human Experience
citations.description: Comprehensive guide to 87 emotions and experiences, providing language and understanding for the full spectrum of human feelings.

citations.title: The Feeling Wheel: A Tool for Expanding Awareness of Emotions and Increasing Spontaneity and Intimacy
citations.description: Comprehensive emotion vocabulary tool organizing feelings from core emotions to specific emotional states.

citations.title: Descartes' Error: Emotion, Reason, and the Human Brain
citations.description: Groundbreaking work on the somatic marker hypothesis and the integration of emotion and reason in decision-making.

citations.title: The twenty-item Toronto Alexithymia Scale—I. Item selection and cross-validation of the factor structure
citations.description: Development and validation of the TAS-20, the most widely used assessment tool for alexithymia.

citations.title: Cognitive-emotional processing in alexithymia: an integrative review
citations.description: Integrative review defining alexithymia as a multifaceted personality construct involving difficulty identifying feelings, difficulty describing feelings, and externally oriented thinking.

citations.title: Validity and reliability of the Bermond–Vorst Alexithymia Questionnaire
citations.description: Validation study of the BVAQ, an alternative assessment tool for alexithymia with cognitive and affective subscales.

citations.title: Cambridge Handbook of Alexithymia (Excerpt)
citations.description: Recent academic excerpt from Cambridge University Press covering contemporary alexithymia research and applications.

citations.title: Wherever You Go, There You Are: Mindfulness Meditation in Everyday Life
citations.description: Foundational work on mindfulness practice and its applications to emotional awareness and regulation.

citations.title: The Little Book of Hygge: Danish Secrets to Happy Living
citations.description: Exploration of the Danish concept of hygge and its relationship to contentment and emotional well-being.

citations.title: Investigating alexithymia in autism: A systematic review and meta-analysis
citations.description: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 studies examining alexithymia in autism. Found 49.93% prevalence of alexithymia in autistic individuals compared to 4.89% in neurotypical controls, with a 6.5x increased risk.

citations.title: Age is strongly associated with alexithymia in the general population
citations.description: Large population study (n=5,454) from Finland's Health 2000 Study finding 9.9% prevalence of alexithymia in the general population. Men showed higher rates (11.9%) than women (8.1%). Alexithymia was associated with increasing age, male gender, low education, and poor health.

citations.title: The psychometric assessment of alexithymia: Development and validation of the Perth Alexithymia Questionnaire
citations.description: Development and validation of the Perth Alexithymia Questionnaire (PAQ), a 24-item self-report measure that separately assesses difficulty identifying, difficulty describing, and externally oriented thinking for positive and negative emotions.

citations.title: The Development of the Toronto Structured Interview for Alexithymia: Item Selection, Factor Structure, Reliability and Concurrent Validity
citations.description: Development and validation of the Toronto Structured Interview for Alexithymia (TSIA), a clinician-administered interview measure of alexithymia.

citations.title: An observer scale to measure alexithymia
citations.description: Development and validation of the 33-item Observer Alexithymia Scale (OAS), an informant-rated measure of alexithymia.

citations.title: The Levels of Emotional Awareness Scale: A Cognitive-Developmental Measure of Emotion
citations.description: Development and validation of the Levels of Emotional Awareness Scale (LEAS), a performance-based measure in which respondents describe emotional reactions to written vignettes, scored for the cognitive complexity and differentiation of the responses.

citations.title: Clarifying the relationship between alexithymia and subjective interoception
citations.description: Open-access study across Italian, U.S., and Singaporean samples (N=814) using self-report measures and machine-learning analyses. It found alexithymia was associated with subjective interoceptive accuracy and attention deficits, especially difficulty identifying and describing feelings, while externally oriented thinking showed weaker links and the BVAQ affective dimension was not associated.

citations.title: An argument for basic emotions
citations.description: Classic statement of basic emotion theory arguing that emotions evolved for adaptive life tasks and have both shared and distinctive features.

citations.title: Basic Emotions, Natural Kinds, Emotion Schemas, and a New Paradigm
citations.description: Nuanced defense of basic emotions that distinguishes biologically basic emotions from broader emotion schemas shaped by cognition and development.

citations.title: The theory of constructed emotion: an active inference account of interoception and categorization
citations.description: Major statement of the theory of constructed emotion, framing emotion as arising from predictive regulation, interoception, and categorization rather than fixed emotion modules.

citations.title: The Theory of Constructed Emotion: More Than a Feeling
citations.description: Recent article arguing that basic emotion theory and the theory of constructed emotion rest on incompatible assumptions and should not be collapsed into a single compromise view.

citations.title: The brain basis of emotion: A meta-analytic review
citations.description: Meta-analytic review comparing locationist and psychological constructionist accounts of emotion and finding little support for simple one-region/one-emotion mappings.

citations.title: Core affect and the psychological construction of emotion
citations.description: Influential psychological construction account proposing core affect as a basic ingredient of emotion, mood, and other affective states.

citations.title: Emotion, core affect, and psychological construction
citations.description: Review article extending the core affect framework and arguing for a broader psychological construction approach to emotion science.

citations.title: Appraisal Considered as a Process of Multilevel Sequential Checking
citations.description: Foundational appraisal-theory chapter describing the component process model and the sequential checking account of emotion differentiation.
citations.book_title: Appraisal Processes in Emotion: Theory, Methods, Research

citations.title: The Emotion Process: Event Appraisal and Component Differentiation
citations.description: Review of appraisal as an emotion process, focusing on how evaluation of events leads to differentiated emotion components and action tendencies.

citations.title: Selected Principles of Pankseppian Affective Neuroscience
citations.description: Overview of Pankseppian affective neuroscience and its seven primary emotional systems, emphasizing cross-species brain research and deep motivational circuitry.

citations.title: The Biology of Fear
citations.description: A focused biological treatment of fear that illustrates how one emotion category can be investigated with greater mechanistic precision than broad folk taxonomies allow.
